posts.headline: Coordination Framework source says cabinet will be formed within two weeks at most
posts.summary: A source inside the Shi'a Coordination Framework tells the account the bloc is now serious about presenting a Zaidi-led cabinet within two weeks at the latest, conditional on no unforeseen rupture. The framing is the tightest internal-CF timeline aired since the designation. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: Field claim Trump will appoint a presidential representative to Iraq with a hard reform mandate
posts.summary: Account claims Trump is preparing to name a dedicated US presidential representative to Iraq, drawn from operators steeped in Iraqi affairs and described as 'merciful toward none', signalling an imminent intensification of the US push on cabinet-level vetting and corruption files. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: Egyptian commentator Tawfiq Okasha claims Iranian leadership cannot safely hold a Khamenei burial
posts.summary: The account amplifies Egyptian media figure Tawfiq Okasha asserting that surviving Iranian leaders cannot safely conduct a public burial for Ali Khamenei because of targeting risk, framing the post-strike Iranian command as still operationally exposed. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: KDPI ideological positioning called incoherent on the eve of European outreach
posts.summary: The account flags an internal KDPI contradiction, with a party spokesperson denying any ideology while attacking communism and socialism even as the party president meets European social-democratic counterparts and the KDPI itself sits in that political family.

posts.headline: Zaidi published CV lists degrees without naming universities or graduation years
posts.summary: The account flags that Iraq PM-designate Ali al-Zaidi's officially circulated biography lists academic credentials without specifying institutions or graduation dates, and reads the multi-faction Coordination Framework backing rather than a direct proxy relationship as the more accurate description of his sponsorship.

posts.headline: Duhok cleric Mullah Rashid Kochar held five days for criticizing Masrour Barzani and electricity costs
posts.summary: The account reports Mullah Rashid Kochar has been detained for five days in Duhok after criticizing Prime Minister Masrour Barzani and the cost of electricity, part of a wider arrest wave it says has now hit seven to ten activists and journalists in the province.

posts.headline: Call for deep US background checks on incoming Iraqi ministers and deputies
posts.summary: Knights amplifies a warning that the next US engagement on Iraq must run deep background checks on every minister and deputy minister Zaidi proposes, citing the risk of militia-aligned technocrats slipping into sensitive portfolios under the cover of a consensus cabinet.

posts.headline: Back-to-back Iraq Finance Minister and CBI Governor meetings flagged as the live banking-stability axis
posts.summary: Knights amplifies a UticaRisk readout describing back-to-back working sessions with Iraq's Finance Minister and Central Bank Governor on the cash crunch and dollar-clearing window, framing the MoF-CBI axis as the file Zaidi inherits with the highest stability sensitivity.

posts.headline: Hormuz market-recovery period likely to run months to over a year even after a reopening
posts.summary: Halabi amplifies a Middle East Council assessment arguing the post-closure recovery period for Hormuz oil and shipping markets will plausibly stretch from months into more than a year once a reopening is locked in, given the cargo backlog, insurance pricing and rerouting overhang.
